attempt: Transfer TICKET63's rule: a stable separator must become an explicit automaton table with transition closure and collision tests before it can support any theorem claim.

bounded_result.automaton_gate: exact-gap separator states need an explicit sieve-level transition automaton
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: A finite twin-prime sieve automaton covers every sieve-level lift with retained gap-2 mass, or a parity collision produces a stable exact-gap mass leak.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: two sieve-level lifts with the same parity automaton state but conflicting exact-gap labels
bounded_result.frontier_rule: The next useful artifact is an explicit state table plus a proof of closure or a first collision. Heuristic separator stability is not enough.

obstruction: This is a transfer discipline, not a solution. It raises the target from bounded separator survival to automaton closure.
candidate_theorem: A finite twin-prime sieve automaton covers every sieve-level lift with retained gap-2 mass, or a parity collision produces a stable exact-gap mass leak.
next_experiment: Build the problem-specific automaton table and quotient/collision audit.
claim_boundary: No twin prime proof and no certified twin prime counterexample.
